name: Mean Reversion Fade
summary: Fade an overextended move once price sweeps an extreme, rejects it, and re-accepts back toward value.
marketConditions: Price extends materially away from the mean into a defined deviation zone.
Liquidity is fading or sweep-and-reject behavior appears at the extreme.
No fresh catalyst is forcing trend continuation.

context: Mark the session mean, VWAP, and prior swing extremes before entry.
Only fade when price is extended into known liquidity or deviation zones.
Prefer first clean rejection after a sweep, not the third or fourth attempt.
setup: Price tags outer band, prior high or low, or deviation threshold.
Rejection wick or failed auction appears at the extreme.
Momentum slows and closes stop progressing in trend direction.
trigger: Enter on reclaim back inside the prior range after the sweep.
Use lower-high or higher-low break as execution trigger.
Scale only if the rejection structure tightens risk.
confirmation: Stretch or displacement is visibly extended.
Extreme is rejected with wick imbalance or failed breakout.
Target path back to mean is open and not blocked immediately by chop.
invalidation: Price accepts outside the deviation zone with consecutive closes.
Trend accelerates after the rejection instead of stalling.
New session expansion resets the regime.
noTrade: High-impact news just reset the market regime.
The mean is too close to justify at least planned reward.
You are fading against strong multi-timeframe momentum without exhaustion.

stopLoss: Place stop beyond sweep high or low or beyond the deviation band.
Keep stop outside obvious noise, not inside the rejection wick midpoint.
Cancel the trade if acceptable size requires excessive stop distance.
takeProfit: First target is the local mean, VWAP, or middle of range.
Second target can be the opposite side of balance if reversal strengthens.
Lock partials quickly because fades lose edge when mean is reclaimed.
management: Reduce risk once price returns to mean.
Do not turn a failed fade into a swing hold.
Re-exit if price stalls at midrange and cannot continue.
timeExit: Close if reversion does not start within 2 to 5 candles after entry.
Flatten before active session reopen if the edge was session-specific.
Do not hold a mean-reversion trade once trend conditions replace range conditions.
earlyExit: Price reclaims the extreme and holds beyond it.
Mean is touched but price cannot continue through balance.
A fresh impulse candle invalidates the rejection narrative.

sizing: Risk less on countertrend fades than on trend-following trades.
Size from the extreme-to-stop distance, not from the hope of a snapback.
Do not add size to a fading trade unless a second rejection creates a new plan.
exposureLimits: One fade thesis per symbol at a time.
Stop trading fades after two same-session failures.
Skip if the setup conflicts with major regime shift news.

journaling: Record the deviation or stretch metric used.
Note whether the trade faded a sweep, a band, or a prior high or low.
Review how quickly price returned to mean after entry.
assistantPrompts: What defines overextended for you: deviation, ATR multiple, VWAP distance, or structure?
Do you exit fully at the mean or keep a runner to the other side of range?
Which session windows make you trust or avoid a fade?
